steps.title: Read the praise in the images
steps.content: The speaker addresses Dunbar admiringly and calls attention to his "mighty pen." The poem praises his ability to understand and represent human experience, including hearts and souls, while also noticing natural details such as flowers and a brook.

steps.title: Keep read figurative
steps.content: In context, "read" does not mean ordinary literal reading. It means Dunbar can perceive people and nature deeply enough to write about them with insight. The question asks for that broad characterization of the speaker's praise.

steps.title: Reject literal or absent claims
steps.content: Choice B misreads "read" as literal reading, Choice C adds an unsupported writing process, and Choice D invents a shared afternoon in nature. The remaining choice captures the praise for Dunbar's perceptive writing. The answer is A.
